data.title: Nemont Stock Drops Ahead Of Q1 Earnings
data.description:
• Newmont stock is under selling pressure. Why is NEM stock retreating?

Expectations Build Ahead Of Newmont's Q1 Report

Wall Street expects earnings of $2.19 per share on $6.43 billion in revenue, but despite this, the setup heading into the print is seemingly being shaped more by last quarter's exceptionally strong performance and the high bar it created, than by the headline numbers expected today.

In its fourth‑quarter update, Newmont described 2025 as "a milestone year", highlighting record free cash flow, aggressive debt reduction and billions returned to shareholders. With the stock already pricing in a strong operational backdrop, traders are trimming exposure ahead of tonight's results.

Q4 Strength Set the Stage For A High‑Stakes Q1

Newmont's fourth‑quarter report was one of its strongest in years. Management emphasized that the company delivered $7.3 billion in free cash flow, generated $3.6 billion from portfolio optimization and returned $3.4 billion to shareholders. The company also reduced debt by $3.4 billion, ending the year in a net cash position of $2.1 billion.

Operationally, Newmont met its full‑year production and cost guidance, producing 5.9 million gold ounces, 28 million ounces of silver and 135 thousand tonnes of copper. The company also declared commercial production at Ahafo North and approved the Lihir mine life extension, unlocking access to more than 5 million future gold ounces.

Strategic Momentum Is Shaping The Setup

Newmont's long‑term positioning remains strong. The company ended 2025 with 118.2 million ounces of gold reserves, significant copper and silver exposure, and a refreshed capital allocation framework designed to support predictable dividend growth. Management also highlighted a focus on margin expansion, robust free cash flow and disciplined investment across its world‑class portfolio.

But with 2026 guidance calling for 5.3 million gold ounces and higher AISC levels, traders are watching closely to see whether cost pressures or production timing could soften the near‑term outlook.

Newmont Technical Analysis

Newmont is sitting in the upper half of its 52-week range ($48.27 to $134.88), which keeps the longer-term uptrend in view even as momentum cools from earlier highs. The stock is trading 2.1% below its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 1.0% below its 100-day SMA, a setup that leans toward short-term hesitation rather than a clean trend higher.

The moving average convergence divergence (MACD), a trend/momentum measure, has the MACD line below the signal line and the histogram is negative, which points to fading upside momentum versus the prior push. That lines up with the stock's current posture under the 20-day and 50-day averages, where rallies can have a harder time sticking.

Over the last 12 months, the stock is up 106.53%, which shows the bigger-picture trend has been strong despite the current consolidation. With a swing high and swing low both set in March, traders often treat the post-March range as the near-term "decision zone" for the next directional move.

  • Key Resistance: $122.00 — a level where upside attempts have tended to stall.
  • Key Support: $94.50 — an area where buyers have previously shown up.

NEM Shares Are Dipping

NEM Price Action: Newmont shares were down 2.63% at $108.91 at the time of publication on Thursday, according to Benzinga Pro.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: Why Is Broadcom Stock Down On Thursday?
data.description:
Broadcom Inc. (NASDAQ:AVGO) shares are trading slightly lower on Thursday. This follows a pivotal disclosure regarding the company’s hardware pipeline.

The Nasdaq is up 0.05% while the S&P 500 has shed 0.01%.

• Why are AVGO shares trading down?

Long-Term AI Partnership Extension

Custom Silicon Drives Growth

Broadcom will also provide networking components for Google's AI data racks. Google is now splitting TPUs into separate processors for training and inference. Broadcom plays a central role in this custom silicon push.

Management Highlights Efficiency

Google CEO Sundar Pichai emphasized scaling AI cost-effectively. Senior Vice President Amin Vahdat stated the shift matches evolving AI workloads.

Broadcom Stock: Key Levels and Trends to Watch

Broadcom is pressing into fresh 52-week highs after setting a prior peak in April.

The stock is trading 21.2% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 24.4% above its 100-day SMA, which leans toward a firmly bullish short- and intermediate-term trend.

The relative strength index (RSI), a momentum gauge, is 79.12, which signals overbought conditions and a higher chance of short-term pullbacks or sideways digestion.

The 50-day SMA crossed above the 200-day SMA in April (a golden cross), reinforcing that the longer-term trend has flipped back in favor of bulls after the death cross in March. Over the last 12 months, the stock has been up 138.23%.

AVGO Stock Price Activity: Broadcom shares were down 0.27% at $421.51 at the time of publication on Thursday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image via Shutterstock

data.title: What's Pushing Nebius Shares Higher Thursday?
data.description:
Nebius Group N.V. Class A Ordinary Shares (NASDAQ:NBIS) shares are trading higher on Thursday, as buyers keep leaning into high-momentum names even with the major indexes split.

  • Nebius Group stock is moving in positive territory. Why is NBIS stock trading higher?

Nebius Stays Close To Recent Highs

Nebius is still within roughly 8% of its April high at $168.71, keeping breakout-watch levels in play as the stock extends a 677% run over the past year and traders keep breakout levels on the radar.

Thursday's push looks tied to continued technical strength, with the stock holding near its recent highs and staying well above key moving averages. With Utilities (XLU) up 2.31% and Industrials (XLI) up 1.84%, the tape is favoring leadership and trend-following setups more than broad-based buying.

Market breadth is slightly negative (advance/decline ratio: 0.8), so NBIS trading green stands out as a relative-strength move. That matters because it suggests dip-buyers are still active in this name even when more sectors are red than green (5 advancing vs. 6 declining).

Nebius has also shown stock-specific demand recently, outperforming a session where the Nasdaq was down 0.57% and the S&P 500 was down 0.36%. That kind of relative strength often keeps momentum traders engaged even when index-level flows are mixed.

Shares Trade Above Key Averages

Nebius is still pinned near the top of its 52-week range after setting a 52-week high in April, which is consistent with a market that's rewarding strong uptrends. The stock is trading 20.2% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 52.8% above its 100-day SMA, which indicates the short-term trend and the intermediate trend are both still pointed up.

The moving average structure also stays constructive, with the 20-day SMA above the 50-day SMA and the 50-day SMA above the 200-day SMA, a setup that typically aligns with sustained trend demand. With the next clear ceiling near $168.50, traders are watching whether rallies keep getting accepted near the prior peak area.

MACD (moving average convergence divergence), a trend/momentum measure, is above its signal line and the histogram is positive, which leans toward upside momentum staying in control. When MACD is above the signal line, it usually means buyers have had the stronger hand lately, even if the pace can cool.

  • Key Resistance: $168.50 — near the recent ceiling where upside attempts have stalled.
  • Key Support: $132.00 — near the 20-day SMA zone where pullbacks have recently found support.

Earnings & Analyst Outlook

Looking further out, the next major catalyst for the stock arrives with the May 19, 2026 (estimated) earnings report.

  • EPS Estimate: Loss of 73 cents (Down from a loss of 39 cents YoY)
  • Revenue Estimate: $374.23 million (Up from $55.30 million YoY)
  • Valuation: P/E of 1362.3x (Indicates premium valuation relative to peers)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $165.30. Recent analyst moves include:

  • Wolfe Research: Initiated with Peer Perform (April 16)
  • Cantor Fitzgerald: Initiated with Overweight (Target $129.00) (April 9)
  • B of A Securities: Initiated with Buy (Target $150.00) (March 24)

Shares Lift On Thursday

NBIS Stock Price Activity: Nebius Group shares were up 1.09% at $157.84 at the time of publication on Thursday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: Dow Shares Fall On Middle East Supply Chain Impact
data.description:
• Dow stock is among today’s weakest performers. Why are DOW shares down?

Earnings Snapshot

Revenue fell 6% year-over-year to $9.79 billion, slightly missing the $9.646 billion estimate.

The chemical giant reported an adjusted loss of 14 cents per share, beating Wall Street’s projected 29-cent loss, according to consensus estimates.

Overall, volume fell 2% Y/Y, on declines in Industrial Intermediates & Infrastructure due to the Middle East conflict. This was due to lower merchant olefins sales in Europe, the Middle East, Africa, and India (EMEAI), following the idling of a regional cracker earlier in the year. Local price declined Y/Y in the quarter.

Jim Fitterling, Dow chair and CEO, said, “In the first quarter, our results reflect the growing impact of Dow’s self-help actions. Additionally, the margin backdrop began to positively inflect in March following global supply constraints, as impacts from the conflict in the Middle East quickly became widespread.”

Profitability and Cash Flow

Dow posted a GAAP net loss of $445 million for the quarter, compared to a loss of $290 million in the same quarter a year ago.

Additionally, operating EBIT stood at $154 million, down from $230 million in the same period a year ago.

Operating cash flow from continuing operations was $1.12 billion, an increase of $1.0 billion Y/Y, aided by working capital improvement and receipt of payment from NOVA Chemicals.

The company returned $252 million to shareholders through dividends in the quarter.

Fitterling added, “We are already seeing rapid positive momentum from our announced pricing actions in every business and every region, as well as constructive impacts to our operating rates.”

Weak Sales Across Segment

Packaging & Specialty Plastics revenue fell 7% Y/Y to $4.9 billion, as local prices fell 9% Y/Y, on weaker polyethylene prices.

Also, Industrial Intermediates & Infrastructure revenue declined 8% Y/Y to $2.6 billion, due to a decline across both businesses.

Performance Materials & Coatings revenue remained flat Y/Y at $2.1 billion. Local price dipped 4% Y/Y owing to a decline in Coatings & Performance Monomers.

Outlook & Conference Call Takeaways

Dow sees second-quarter 2026 net revenue of $12 billion versus the consensus of $11.07 billion and EBITDA of $2 billion, led by improved pricing and higher asset utilization.

Dow continues to focus on its "Transform to Outperform" program and internal efficiency initiatives and delivered $193 million in cost savings in the first quarter. The company targets about $2 billion in near-term EBITDA uplift from the initiatives.

Also, Dow identified over $400 million (of the committed $1.3 billion) in productivity improvements from site transformation programs across ~25% of large sites.

The company also announced leadership changes, with Karen S. Carter set to assume the CEO role in July.

Dow expects supply chain disruptions linked to the Middle East conflict to continue through 2026.

The company’s planned shutdowns of European assets are expected to start delivering benefits in 2026, including the reduction of roughly 25% of siloxane production capacity in the region.

Management reiterated its commitment to delivering a 5%+ earnings growth path, supported by cycle resilience and ongoing pricing recovery through 2026.

DOW Price Action: Dow shares were down 3.80% at $37.34 at the time of publication on Thursday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo via Shutterstock

data.title: What's Behind The Jump In Pineapple Financial Inc. Stock?
data.description:
• Pineapple Financial stock is showing exceptional strength. Why is PAPL stock up today?

Pineapple Financial Expands Share Buyback to $15 Million

Pineapple Financial's decision to expand its share repurchase program to $15 million is the kind of shareholder-friendly move that does two things at once: it signals management's willingness to support the stock, and it can tighten the available float.

Traders see a buyback headline and immediately start gaming the second-order effects: fewer shares available, more sensitivity to incremental demand, and a higher probability that momentum feeds on itself. That's how you get a session where the stock becomes its own ecosystem—less correlated to the indices and more driven by positioning, liquidity, and who's willing to chase.

PAPL'S Tape: Short-Term Heat, Longer-Term Scars

Thursday's surge didn't erase the longer-term damage, but it did spotlight a stock trying to rebuild a trend after a steep drawdown from last year's highs. PAPL is trading 39.7% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) while still sitting 30.8% below its 100-day SMA—a split personality that screams "near-term traction" while admitting the intermediate-term repair job isn't finished.

The structure under the hood reinforces that tension. The 20-day SMA remains below the 50-day SMA, which keeps the near-term trend from fully resetting bullish even after the pop. And the January death cross—50-day SMA below the 200-day SMA—still hangs over the chart as a reminder that the bigger-picture trend has been under pressure for months.

Momentum, however, is trying to make its case. The moving average convergence divergence (MACD) is above its signal line and the histogram is positive, leaning toward improving momentum rather than a quick fade. The market's message: the stock may still be in rehab, but it's currently sprinting on the treadmill.

Big Swings, Wide Range, And A Lot Of Overhead Supply

Zoom out and the volatility becomes the story, not the exception. PAPL is up 228.37% over the past 12 months—less a smooth uptrend than a reminder that this ticker can move violently in both directions. It's also far below the 52-week high of $9.53, yet well above the 52-week low of 7 cents, which frames the current setup as a rebound off the floor with plenty of overhead supply waiting to sell into strength.

From a levels perspective, traders have clear markers. Resistance sits at $1.50, a round-number zone near the 100-day SMA area where rallies can stall. Support is around 50 cents, near the 20-day/50-day area where buyers have recently defended pullbacks. In a float-sensitive name, those levels can matter less as "fundamentals" and more as crowd behavior—where conviction shows up, and where it disappears.

Shares Soar On Thursday

PAPL Stock Price Activity: Pineapple Financial shares were up 58.29% at $0.94 at the time of publication on Thursday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image: Shutterstock
